channel.title: Economy
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Canada’s prime minister says economic ties with US are a weakness that must be corrected
item.description: “We have to take care of ourselves because we can’t rely on one foreign partner,” Mark Carney said in a video address. “We can’t control the disruption coming from our neighbors."

media:content.media:title: Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney, center, arrives to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, China, Friday, Jan. 16, 2026. (AP Photo/Vincent Thian, Pool)

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: Trump Keeps Gambling With the Economy — And Getting Away With It
item.description: President Donald Trump has taken one risk after another that could have destabilized the American economy. Iran is the latest crisis to test U.S. economic resilience.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump has launched an attack on Iran, a scenario that has long been the clearest and most direct threat to one of his favored political barometers: gas prices.

item.dc:creator: By Victoria Guida

item.title: Capitol agenda: GOP bets on immigration fight, not big bills
item.dc:creator: By Mia McCarthy and Calen Razor
item.dc:contributor: By Mia McCarthy and Calen Razor
